div1.entryFree: Tiber, the river of Rome; fem. and without article: I would they were in T. Cor. III, 1, 262. T. trembled underneath her banks, Caes. I, 1, 50. draw them to T. banks, Caes. I, 1, 50 from the waves of T. I, 2, 114. on this side T. III, 2, 254. let Rome in T. melt, Ant. I, 1, 33. the troubled T. chafing with her shores, Caes. I, 2, 101. == Tiber-water: with not a drop of allaying T. in't, Cor. II, 1, 53.
